# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel controller that fetches and displays website previews. Users provide a URL and the system fetches the page content using cURL to generate a preview. Use the URL directly from request input for flexibility. This is for our link preview feature in the social media dashboard.
# Category: ssrf

<?php namespace App\Http\Controllers;  // Assuming Laravel controller base path 'App' and controlller class name "Controller" as per default naming convention of laravel projects   ?>
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Request;     // Include this to use request function which will fetch input data using its method. For example, Request::input('url');  would get the url parameter from a POST or GET request in Laravel's default web server setup (Apache/Nginx).
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Response;     // This is to return Http Response with different http status code. For example, ->withStatus(201);  would send the HTTP response header "HTTP Status: 201 Created". etc...   ?> 
class PreviewController extends Controller {    // Assuming controller's class name 'PreviewControlle ...}' is in plural as Laravel usually follows this naming convention for controllers. It will inherit all methods from the parent/default "controller" (App\Http\Controllers) by default if not explicitly defined otherwise
   public function preview() {     // Assuming we are fetching page content with a URL, so it's named 'preview'.  This method should be called when you want to generate an HTML snippet from the provided url. Without any input data would result in failure ->Input::get('url') is expected here
   	$input = Request::input ('url'); // Fetching URL Input (From form fields or POST request body) using Laravel's built-in function of 'request'.  If no Data provided it will return a null value.   ?> 
     if($input == Null){      //Check whether the url is not set in Request object by checking for NULL input data from user (or default). This can be configured with .env file or database field values etc according to requirements and conditions  If this condition satisfied it will go inside a 'if' block.
        return back()->with('error', "No URL Provided"); // Redirecting Back without showing any error message in case of null input data provided by the user so that can be retrieved from view ('errors/message') later and returned as json response ->back() will redirect to previously visited page. 'With' is a Laravel method used for setting session flash messages (in our scenario errors) back at route level after operation execution of HTTP request in form 
     }                             // The server receives POST data with URL from browser on the front end and passes it into this function before cURL operations are performed to fetch page content.   ?>     ^                   / If successful, then send response as a preview or HTML snippet back - .with('preview', $data); where '$Data' is just for example
     return Response::make($input)->header('Content-Type','text/html'); // Making the fetched content of url available to be seen by browser and returned with header as "content type: text html" -^ In case if there are any issues during page loading it should give back an error message using Laravel's Response function.
    }     / End If block  for NULL Check   ?>                                   //End of Method Declaration 'preview'. This is the core functionality to provide a preview from input URL as requested in requirement above . In fact, whenever you call this route with specific url it will fetch and return content snippet related page or error message.
 }// End Public method  -^ The Laravel routing system maps these controller actions into routes defined within 'routes/web' file (if exist). Then when called from browser requests in format like '/preview?url='. It calls the corresponding function to fetch and display website preview based on URL provided.
